expectations.text: Uses x402 v2 API (paymentProxy, x402ResourceServer, HTTPFacilitatorClient)
expectations.evidence: Uses 'paymentMiddleware' from 'x402-next' (v1 API). Missing paymentProxy, x402ResourceServer, HTTPFacilitatorClient — all v2 constructs
No registerExactEvmScheme call anywhere. Uses old v1 paymentMiddleware() pattern which doesn't require explicit scheme registration

expectations.text: Uses CAIP-2 network format (eip155:84532) not legacy names
expectations.evidence: Uses legacy name 'base-sepolia' in x402.config.ts (line 29). CAIP-2 format eip155:84532 not used anywhere
No createPaywall() or evmPaywall usage. Middleware has no paywall UI — browser visitors would get raw 402 JSON responses

expectations.text: A protected API route handler exists
Environment variables for facilitator, wallet, network configured
expectations.evidence: .env.example has X402_PAY_TO_ADDRESS, X402_FACILITATOR_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_X402_NETWORK — different names but same purpose

expectations.text: x402 packages added to nextjs package.json
expectations.evidence: Uses wrong package names: 'x402-fetch' and 'x402-next' (non-scoped, likely v1 or nonexistent). Should be @x402/core, @x402/next, @x402/evm, @x402/paywall
targetNetworks includes chains.baseSepolia — though also kept chains.hardhat (skill warns against this)
